outline_notes: Quantify cumulative memory loss: at chapter 4 start, 45% of original memories are gone; veil spread speed +35%.
Elisa’s scar becomes a living sigil that pulses brighter each stroke; its pattern hints at the missing half of the name.
Name‑Eater’s rasp echoes literally when a name is spoken; the audio cue should sync with the silence that follows.
The Mirror Fruit reflection should show a fleeting image of Elisa’s voice as a spectral overlay, foreshadowing its power.
End with a cliff: the Vault of Glass opens to reveal the Mirror Fruit, but the chorus swallows the corridor, leaving the location ambiguous.
Cumulative memory loss metric set to 45% at chapter start; after Elisa’s voice sacrifice it rises to 52%, accelerating veil spread +35%.
Half‑sigil now fully illuminated when reflected in the Mirror Fruit; missing half hinted to reside within the echo‑phage’s memory‑drain.
Mirror Fruit introduced as a unique glass fruit; its reflective surface can capture a lost memory but requires a voice‑sacrifice to activate.
Name‑Eater’s sonic rasp established as a sensory signature tied to spoken names; can be nullified by silent communication.
Ancient keeper’s pulse recorded as a low thrum in the crypt, setting up its future emergence.

vote_summary.reason: Review fallback applied: OpenRouter request timed out after 300s for model openai/gpt-oss-120b:free.

architect.chapter_goal: Force Elisa to confront the true cost of her remaining strokes, drive the chorus into a crescendo of hostile memory‑phages, and inject a new, paradoxical thread that threatens to collapse the veil’s own logic.
architect.theme_progress: Grief becomes agency: each erased memory gives Elisa more power, yet strips her of identity. Ambition now demands she trade her soul for mastery, while the chorus whispers that the very act of erasing may undo the veil.
arc_targets: Show the veil accelerating as cumulative memory loss passes the 60% tipping point.
Introduce a paradoxical echo‑phage that feeds on the loss of identity itself, turning Elisa’s forgotten self into a hostile horror.
Amplify the chorus into a symphonic threat: every stanza spoken fuels a new wave of echo‑phages that devour the fabric of reality.
Deliver a brutal consequence of the voice sacrifice: Elisa’s personality fractures, leaving her a hollow shell who can still write but cannot remember her own name.

emotional_targets: Intensify Elisa’s sense of betrayal by her own memories
Heighten the chorus as a living, oppressive presence
Create an existential dread that borders on cosmic erasure

character_notes.name: Elisa
character_notes.desire: to wield the Veil yet keep her identity intact
character_notes.fear: to be consumed by the chorus and lose her sense of self
character_notes.contradiction: her drive to re‑write reality clashes with the knowledge that each stroke erases her past
character_notes.secret_pressure: the fragment of the chorus whispering *Sa‑ri…*—a name she knows only as a half‑memory that could either free or damn her

character_notes.name: Maeron
character_notes.desire: to help Elisa secure power for his own future
character_notes.fear: that the Mirror Fruit will betray him by siphoning his hidden memories
character_notes.contradiction: his greed is tempered by an unspoken respect for the Quill’s sanctity
character_notes.secret_pressure: his own forgotten lineage, hinted at by the ink‑etched map, may be the missing half of the sigil

character_notes.name: The Ancient Keeper
character_notes.desire: to restore the Quill’s original purpose
character_notes.fear: to be erased by the chorus’s

dialogue_instructions: Let the chorus speak in fragmented, whispered cadences that break into a sobbing chorus of forgotten sentences.
When Elisa speaks, make her voice echo but fade—each syllable feels like a phantom reminiscence.
Use silence as a character: when the chorus swells, words fall silent, and characters listen for the next breath.
conflict_upgrades: The chorus now produces an Echo‑Phage that devours the very memory of the speaker’s name, leaving them nameless yet capable of writing.
Introduce a paradoxical rule: the more Elisa forgets, the Veil’s power grows—her losses fuel her strokes.
Unveil that the missing half‑sigil is embedded within the chorus’s memory‑drain; to retrieve it, Elisa must willingly surrender all memories, risking total annihilation.

chapter_planner.chapter_hook: Elisa stands before the cathedral’s fractured dome, ink dripping from her fingertips like a dying heart, while the chorus swells into a deafening choir of the forgotten.
arc_payoffs: Elisa’s final stroke shatters the chorus’s core, revealing the echo‑phage’s true hunger.
The veil’s expansion is halted, but at the cost of Elisa’s last memory of her mother’s lullaby.
A new paradoxical thread is planted: the missing half‑sigil is hidden in the echo‑phage’s memory‑drain.
next_chapter_seed: The echo‑phage now feeds on the very identity Elisa has tried to protect.
The ancient keeper’s silence grows louder as the veil’s lattice fractures.
A fragment of the chorus whispers a new, forbidden word that could rewrite the Quill itself.

scene_cards.goal: Show the veil accelerating past the 60% memory‑loss tipping point.
scene_cards.conflict: The cathedral’s walls bleed ink, and the chorus’s whispers echo like a storm, threatening to drown Elisa’s thoughts.
scene_cards.turn: Elisa realizes that each stroke she has made has fed the chorus; she must decide whether to write one final line or let the chorus consume her.
scene_cards.image: Ink splattering across a stone altar, turning the marble into a living river of black.

scene_cards.goal: Introduce the paradoxical echo‑phage that devours the forgotten self.
scene_cards.conflict: The chorus morphs into a creature that feeds on the very memories Elisa has lost, turning her fading self into a hostile horror.
scene_cards.turn: Elisa confronts the echo‑phage, realizing that erasing her past has birthed a monster that now seeks her identity.
scene_cards.image: A translucent, shifting silhouette of Elisa, its edges dissolving into ink.

scene_cards.goal: Amplify the chorus into a symphonic threat, each stanza feeding new echo‑phages.
scene_cards.conflict: The chorus’s words grow louder, each syllable spawning a new wave of echo‑phages that shred the walls.
scene_cards.turn: Elisa must silence the chorus by uttering a forgotten word, but the word
